cross_domain_bonus_reason: Both fields reside within life sciences; mechanobiology and epigenomics share investigators, journals, and experimental infrastructure. Distance does not cross 2+ disciplinary boundaries required for +0.5 bonus.

rankings.title: LAD-Embedded Enhancers as Stiffness-Resistant Mechanical Selectivity Filter
evolution_priorities: Refine >90% non-LAD enrichment prediction with proper null model (65% expected by chance; power calculation needed)
Distinguish constitutive LADs (cLADs) from facultative LADs (fLADs) — filter may be strongest for cLADs
Consider CRISPR tethering gain-of-function: force non-LAD TEAD enhancer into LAD via MS2-Lamin fusion

rankings.title: Coordinated Bivalent-to-Active Enhancer Conversion via Dual ECM-Stiffness-Controlled Enzymes (KDM6B + EP300)
evolution_priorities: Add siKDM6B/siKDM6A genetic experiment alongside GSK-J4 to resolve UTX (KDM6A) vs KDM6B specificity at enhancers
Reframe as sequential two-phase model: EP300/H3K27ac deposition first (fast, YAP-dependent), KDM6B/H3K27me3 removal second (slow, transcriptional upregulation)
Address CBP compensation: use dual EP300+CBP inhibition or genetic approaches; A-485 at 3 uM has partial CBP activity

rankings.title: Enhancer-Encoded Mechanical Memory via Self-Reinforcing H3K27ac-BRD4-EP300 Positive Feedback
evolution_priorities: Lower predicted memory half-life to 6-24 hr (mandatory per Critic; H3K27ac turnover is 2-6 hr, 24-72 hr is quantitatively inconsistent)
Add triptolide transcription-inhibitor control at transfer time to distinguish epigenetic memory from transcriptional auto-regulation
Use dBET6 (BET degrader) vs JQ1 (inhibitor) to distinguish BRD4-specific locus retention from global BRD4 inhibition effects; SAHA control is too non-specific

dropped.title: Stiffness-Dependent Super-Enhancer Nucleation via YAP-Recruited BRD4-MED1 Phase Condensates
dropped.drop_reason: Lowest composite (6.00) and unanimous last place in Elo tournament (0-3). Core mechanistic claim substantially pre-empted by Zanconato et al. Nat Med 2018 (YAP/TAZ/TEAD-BRD4 super-enhancer axis in cancer), reducing novelty score to 5/10. Phase separation biology is contested (McSwiggen et al. 2019). Weakest novelty-groundedness combination in the set. H5 offers a more testable and novel question with equal mechanistic specificity.

top_3_mechanisms.H4: Lamin A/C LAD geometry filter — nuclear architecture emergent property
top_3_mechanisms.H2: KDM6B + EP300 dual enzyme coordinated bivalent-to-active conversion
top_3_mechanisms.H5: EP300-BRD4-EP300 positive feedback self-reinforcing circuit for temporal persistence

diversity_check.note: All three advancing hypotheses use distinct primary mechanisms. H2 and H5 share EP300 as a molecular actor but address orthogonal biological questions (bivalent conversion vs temporal memory). No diversity bonus or reordering required.

elo_tournament.note: Elo and linear composite rankings are identical (H4 > H2 > H5 > H1). Pairwise comparisons implicitly weight interpretability of results more heavily than the 6-dimension average. H4's advantage: binary, unambiguous experimental outcome. H1's consistent loss: Zanconato 2018 pre-emption makes it hard to frame as a first-priority experiment.
